text:
Title: Business Travel Policy
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

The purpose of this Business Travel Policy is to establish clear guidelines for planning, approving, booking,
and reimbursing business travel expenses incurred by employees of NovaTech Solutions. As a SaaS product
company with approximately 500 employees operating from Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech Solutions
recognizes that travel may be necessary to support customer engagements, sales activities, conferences,
training programs, business development initiatives, and operational requirements.
This policy ensures that business travel is conducted in a cost-effective, safe, and consistent manner while
maintaining employee comfort and supporting business objectives. Employees are expected to exercise
sound judgment and select reasonable travel options that balance convenience, productivity, and company
cost considerations.

This policy applies to:
• Full-time employees
• Part-time employees traveling on company business
• Managers and Department Heads
• Executive Leadership Team members
• Employees attending customer meetings, conferences, training programs, or company events
Contractors and consultants are governed by travel provisions contained within their individual agreements
unless otherwise approved by the Finance Team.
The policy applies to both domestic and international travel undertaken on behalf of NovaTech Solutions.

Business travel must have a legitimate business purpose and receive appropriate approval before bookings
are made.
Examples of approved travel include:
• Customer meetings
• Sales presentations
• Industry conferences
• Professional training programs
1
• Internal leadership meetings
• Recruitment activities
• Business partner engagements
Travel for personal convenience or non-business purposes will not be reimbursed.

NovaTech Solutions uses a centralized travel approval and booking process to ensure cost control and
compliance.
Standard Travel Workflow

Advance Notice Requirements
Travel Type Minimum Advance Notice
Domestic Travel 7 business days
International Travel 21 business days
Conference Travel 30 calendar days
Executive Travel 5 business days
Employees should submit requests as early as possible to secure favorable pricing.
Booking Responsibilities
• Flights, hotels, and major transportation arrangements must be booked through the designated
Travel Coordinator or Finance Team.
• Employees should not make independent bookings unless prior written authorization is provided.
• Emergency travel may be booked directly by employees with manager approval and supporting
documentation.
Travel requests can be submitted through:
hr-portal.novatech.com
2

Domestic travel includes travel within India for business purposes.
Employees are expected to:
• Use the most economical reasonable travel option.
• Schedule meetings efficiently to minimize travel costs.
• Select hotels within approved limits.
• Use company-approved transportation providers whenever possible.
Domestic travel generally requires approval from the Direct Manager and Department Head.
Travel lasting more than five consecutive working days may require additional review by the Finance Team.

International travel requires additional review due to increased costs and compliance considerations.
Employees traveling internationally must:
• Obtain approval from the Department Director or Vice President.
• Ensure passport validity extends at least six months beyond travel dates.
• Obtain required visas and travel documentation.
• Complete any required security or compliance training before departure.
• Follow applicable export control and data security requirements.
International travel exceeding ₹300,000 in estimated total cost requires approval from the Chief Financial
Officer (CFO).
NovaTech Solutions may require travel risk assessments for travel to high-risk locations.

Employees traveling overnight may be eligible for daily meal and incidental allowances.
The following standard per diem rates apply:
City Category Examples Per Diem Rate
Bangalore, Hyderabad, Mumbai, Delhi,
Tier 1 Metro Cities ₹2,500 per day
Chennai
Tier 2 Cities Pune, Ahmedabad, Kochi, Chandigarh, Jaipur ₹2,000 per day
3
City Category Examples Per Diem Rate
Tier 3 Cities All other domestic locations ₹1,500 per day
International Travel - Asia Pacific Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Bangkok ₹5,000 per day
International Travel - Europe London, Paris, Berlin, Amsterdam ₹8,000 per day
International Travel - North ₹10,000 per
New York, San Francisco, Toronto
America day
Per diem allowances cover:
• Meals
• Local transportation not separately reimbursed
• Incidental business expenses
Receipts are generally not required for per diem claims unless specifically requested by the Finance Team.

Employees are expected to select reasonably priced accommodations that are safe, professional, and
conveniently located.
Domestic Hotel Limits
Employee Level Maximum Nightly Rate
Individual Contributor ₹8,000
Team Lead ₹10,000
Manager ₹12,000
Director and Above ₹15,000
International Hotel Limits
Employee Level Maximum Nightly Rate
Individual Contributor ₹15,000
Team Lead ₹18,000
Manager ₹22,000
Director and Above ₹30,000
Hotel rates exceeding these limits require prior written approval from the Finance Team.
4
Luxury hotels or premium room upgrades are generally not reimbursable unless justified by business
requirements.

Employees are expected to travel using the most cost-effective flight option appropriate for the journey.
Domestic Flights
Employee Level Approved Class
All Employees Economy Class
International Flights
Employee Level Approved Class
Individual Contributor Economy Class
Team Lead Economy Class
Manager Premium Economy (Flights over 8 hours)
Director and Above Business Class (Flights over 8 hours)
Employees may choose upgraded travel at personal expense.
Additional baggage fees will only be reimbursed when required for business purposes.

Approved transportation methods include:
• Airport taxis
• Ride-sharing services
• Metro and train services
• Rental cars when justified by business needs
Employees should select economical transportation options whenever practical.
Luxury transportation services are generally not reimbursable without prior approval.

All travel-related expenses must be reported promptly following completion of travel.
5
Expense Reporting Process

section_title: 5. Submit for Finance Team review and reimbursement.
text:
Submission Deadline
Employees must submit all travel expense reports within 15 calendar days after returning from travel.
Claims submitted more than 30 calendar days after travel may be denied unless exceptional circumstances
are approved by the Finance Manager.

Receipts are required for:
• Airfare
• Hotel accommodations
• Ground transportation exceeding ₹500
• Conference registrations
• Business meals exceeding ₹1,000
• Any individual expense exceeding ₹500
Digital receipts and electronic invoices are acceptable if clearly legible.
Employees should retain copies of all supporting documentation until reimbursement is completed.

Examples of policy violations include:
• Booking travel without required approvals
• Exceeding spending limits without authorization
• Submitting inaccurate expense reports
• Claiming personal expenses as business expenses
• Falsifying receipts or documentation
Violations may result in reimbursement denial, disciplinary action, repayment obligations, or termination of
employment in cases involving fraud or deliberate misconduct.
6

This policy will be reviewed annually by the Finance Team, HR Department, and Executive Leadership Team
to ensure alignment with business requirements, travel patterns, and cost management objectives.
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to modify this policy at any time and will communicate significant
updates through official company channels.

For questions regarding travel approvals, booking procedures, expense reporting, or reimbursement status,
employees should contact:
hr@novatech.com
7

Title: Employee Benefits Guide
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

NovaTech Solutions is committed to providing a comprehensive and competitive benefits program that
supports the health, financial wellbeing, professional growth, and long-term success of employees. As a
SaaS product company with approximately 500 employees across Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech
Solutions believes that investing in employees contributes directly to organizational success and employee
satisfaction.
This Employee Benefits Guide provides an overview of the benefits available to eligible employees, including
health insurance, retirement benefits, performance incentives, learning opportunities, wellness programs,
stock options, and referral rewards.
The benefits described in this document may be modified periodically based on business needs, legal
requirements, and market conditions.

This guide applies to:
• Full-time permanent employees
• Employees who have completed onboarding requirements
• Eligible employees enrolled in company-sponsored benefit programs
Certain benefits may have eligibility requirements based on employment status, tenure, job level, or
performance criteria.

NovaTech Solutions provides comprehensive group health insurance coverage to eligible employees.
Standard Coverage
Covered Member Sum Insured
Employee ₹5,00,000
Employee + Spouse ₹7,50,000
Employee + Spouse + Children ₹10,00,000
1
Covered Member Sum Insured
Family Floater (Parents Optional) Up to ₹15,00,000
Coverage Highlights
• Cashless hospitalization at network hospitals
• Pre- and post-hospitalization expenses
• Day-care procedures
• Ambulance expenses
• Maternity benefits
• Newborn coverage
• Annual health check-ups
Additional Coverage
Employees may purchase enhanced coverage for dependents through payroll deductions during the annual
benefits enrollment period.
Health insurance coverage becomes effective from the employee's date of joining, subject to insurer
requirements.

NovaTech Solutions complies with applicable Provident Fund regulations and contributes toward employee
retirement savings.
PF Contributions
Contribution Type Percentage
Employee Contribution 12% of Basic Salary
Employer Contribution 12% of Basic Salary
PF deductions and contributions are processed monthly through payroll.
Employees may:
• View PF contributions through payroll statements.
• Access PF balances through government-authorized portals.
• Transfer PF accounts from previous employers.
• Nominate beneficiaries according to statutory requirements.
The HR and Payroll Teams provide assistance with PF-related documentation and transfers.
2

Eligible employees may receive gratuity benefits in accordance with applicable labor laws.
Eligibility
Employees become eligible for gratuity upon:
• Completion of at least 5 years of continuous service with NovaTech Solutions.
Gratuity Calculation
Gratuity is calculated based on:
• Last drawn Basic Salary
• Years of completed service
• Applicable legal requirements
Payment will be processed following employee separation, retirement, resignation, or other qualifying
events.

NovaTech Solutions rewards high performance through an annual performance bonus program.
Bonus Eligibility
Employees are generally eligible if they:
• Complete at least 6 months of service during the review year.
• Are active employees at the time of payout.
• Meet minimum performance expectations.
Annual Bonus Targets
Performance Rating Target Bonus
Outstanding Up to 20% of Annual Base Salary
Exceeds Expectations Up to 15% of Annual Base Salary
Meets Expectations Up to 10% of Annual Base Salary
Needs Improvement Up to 3% of Annual Base Salary
Unsatisfactory No Bonus
3
Actual bonus payouts may vary based on:
• Individual performance
• Team performance
• Company financial results
• Business objectives achieved
Annual bonuses are typically paid during the first quarter of the following financial year.

NovaTech Solutions encourages continuous learning and professional growth.
Annual Learning Budget
Employee Category Annual Budget
Individual Contributors ₹25,000
Team Leads ₹35,000
Managers ₹50,000
Directors and Above ₹75,000
Eligible expenses include:
• Professional certifications
• Online courses
• Industry conferences
• Technical workshops
• Leadership development programs
• Professional memberships
Employees must obtain manager approval before utilizing the learning budget.
Unused learning budgets do not carry forward to the following year.

Employee wellbeing is an important component of the NovaTech Solutions culture.
Wellness Benefit
Eligible employees receive:
• ₹2,500 per month wellness allowance
4
• Maximum annual benefit of ₹30,000
Approved wellness expenses include:
• Gym memberships
• Fitness classes
• Sports activities
• Mental wellness programs
• Meditation applications
• Ergonomic equipment
• Nutrition counseling
Employees must submit receipts through the expense management system within 30 days of purchase.

NovaTech Solutions offers stock option benefits to eligible employees through its Employee Stock Option
Plan.
ESOP Eligibility
Employees may be considered for stock option grants based on:
• Position level
• Performance history
• Business impact
• Length of service
Typical Grant Guidelines
Employee Level Typical Grant Range
Senior Engineer 500 – 1,500 Options
Team Lead 1,500 – 3,000 Options
Manager 3,000 – 6,000 Options
Director 6,000 – 15,000 Options
Vesting Schedule
Standard vesting follows:
• 4-year vesting period
• 1-year cliff
• Monthly vesting thereafter
Specific grant details are governed by the applicable ESOP agreement and board approvals.
5

NovaTech Solutions encourages employees to help attract high-quality talent through the Employee
Referral Program.
Referral Bonus Structure
Position Level Referral Bonus
Associate / Junior Roles ₹20,000
Mid-Level Professionals ₹40,000
Senior Specialists ₹60,000
Managers ₹75,000
Critical Technical Positions Up to ₹1,00,000
Referral Eligibility Rules

section_title: 4. Employees involved in recruiting decisions are not eligible for referral bonuses related to their own
text:
hiring activities.
Referral bonuses are typically paid in the payroll cycle following successful completion of the eligibility
period.

Employees may also be eligible for additional programs including:
• Annual leave entitlement
• Maternity and paternity leave benefits
• Flexible work arrangements
• Remote work options
• Employee recognition awards
• Team-building activities
• Company-sponsored events
• Complimentary refreshments in office locations
Additional benefits may be introduced periodically based on employee feedback and organizational
priorities.
6

The HR Department administers all employee benefit programs and may request supporting
documentation to verify eligibility.
Employees are responsible for:
• Maintaining accurate personal information.
• Updating dependent details when required.
• Meeting submission deadlines.
• Following program guidelines and approval processes.
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue benefit programs subject to legal
and contractual obligations.

This guide will be reviewed annually by the HR Department, Finance Team, and Executive Leadership Team.
Benefit plans may be updated to reflect regulatory changes, market practices, insurer requirements, and
business objectives.
Employees will be notified of material changes through official company communication channels.

For questions regarding benefits eligibility, claims, enrollments, reimbursements, or program details, please
contact:
hr@novatech.com
7

Title: Employee Code of Conduct
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

The Employee Code of Conduct establishes the standards of professional behavior expected from all
employees of NovaTech Solutions. As a SaaS product company with approximately 500 employees across
Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech Solutions is committed to maintaining a workplace built on integrity,
accountability, innovation, and mutual respect.
This document outlines the principles, expectations, and responsibilities that guide employee conduct in all
business activities. Every employee represents NovaTech Solutions and is expected to uphold the company's
reputation, values, and ethical standards while interacting with colleagues, customers, vendors, partners,
and the public.
Failure to comply with this Code of Conduct may result in disciplinary action as outlined in this policy.

This policy applies to:
• All permanent and probationary employees
• Part-time employees
• Contract workers when engaged in company activities
• Managers, team leads, and executives
• Employees working remotely, from client locations, or from NovaTech Solutions offices
Employees are expected to comply with this policy during working hours, business travel, company-
sponsored events, training programs, and any situation where they represent NovaTech Solutions.

The purpose of this Code of Conduct is to:
• Promote a safe, inclusive, and respectful workplace.
• Ensure ethical decision-making and professional behavior.
• Protect company assets, information, and reputation.
• Maintain compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
• Provide a consistent framework for addressing workplace misconduct.
Every employee shares responsibility for creating a positive and productive work environment.
1

NovaTech Solutions operates according to the following core values:
Core Value Description
Integrity Acting honestly and ethically in all business activities
Accountability Taking responsibility for actions, commitments, and outcomes
Respect Treating colleagues, customers, and partners with dignity
Innovation Encouraging creativity, learning, and continuous improvement
Collaboration Working together to achieve shared goals
Customer Focus Delivering high-quality products and services
Employees are expected to demonstrate these values in daily interactions and business decisions.

All employees must contribute to a professional and respectful workplace.
Employees are expected to:

section_title: 6. Report concerns or violations promptly through appropriate channels.
text:
The following behaviors are prohibited:
• Harassment or discriminatory conduct.
• Threats, violence, or aggressive behavior.
• Use of offensive, abusive, or inappropriate language.
• Deliberate disruption of workplace operations.
• Retaliation against employees who raise concerns in good faith.
NovaTech Solutions maintains a zero-tolerance approach toward workplace harassment and violence.

Employees must avoid situations where personal interests may interfere with the interests of NovaTech
Solutions.
2
Examples of potential conflicts of interest include:
• Holding a significant financial interest in a competitor, vendor, or customer.
• Participating in hiring, promotion, or procurement decisions involving immediate family members.
• Accepting gifts, favors, or benefits that could influence business decisions.
• Performing outside employment that negatively affects job performance or creates competition with
company activities.
Employees must disclose any actual, potential, or perceived conflicts of interest to their Direct Manager and
the HR Manager within five business days of becoming aware of the situation.
The HR Department and relevant department head will review disclosures and determine appropriate
actions.

NovaTech Solutions provides employees with company assets and resources to support business
operations.
Examples include:
• Laptops and desktop computers
• Mobile devices
• Software licenses
• Corporate email accounts
• Cloud services and development environments
• Office facilities and equipment
Employees are expected to:
• Use company resources primarily for legitimate business purposes.
• Protect company property from loss, theft, misuse, or damage.
• Follow information security and data protection requirements.
• Return all company assets upon resignation, termination, or role transfer.
Employees must not install unauthorized software, share confidential information, or use company
resources for unlawful activities.
Any loss, theft, or compromise of company assets must be reported to the IT Service Desk within 24 hours.

NovaTech Solutions respects employees' rights to engage in personal social media activities. However,
employees must exercise good judgment when discussing work-related matters online.
3
Employees must:
• Maintain professionalism when referencing the company.
• Respect confidentiality obligations.
• Avoid sharing proprietary, customer, financial, or internal business information.
• Clearly state that personal opinions are their own when discussing industry topics.
Employees must not:
• Publish confidential company information.
• Represent themselves as official spokespersons without authorization.
• Post content that could damage the reputation of NovaTech Solutions, its customers, or employees.
• Share unreleased product information, customer data, or internal communications.
Official company communications may only be issued by authorized representatives from the Executive
Leadership Team, Marketing Team, or Corporate Communications function.

Employees are encouraged to report suspected violations of this Code of Conduct promptly.
Concerns may be reported to:
• Direct Manager
• HR Manager
• Department Head
• Ethics Committee
• Human Resources Department
Reports made in good faith will be handled confidentially to the extent possible. NovaTech Solutions
prohibits retaliation against any employee who raises a concern or participates in an investigation honestly
and in good faith.

Violations of this Code of Conduct may result in disciplinary action depending on the severity and frequency
of the misconduct.
The standard disciplinary process may include:
Level Action
Level 1 Verbal counseling and coaching
Level 2 Written warning
4
Level Action
Level 3 Final written warning
Level 4 Suspension without pay (up to 10 working days)
Level 5 Termination of employment
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to bypass intermediate steps when serious misconduct occurs,
including fraud, theft, harassment, violence, unauthorized disclosure of confidential information, or
significant security violations.
All disciplinary actions will be documented and maintained in the employee's personnel file by the HR
Department.

This Code of Conduct will be reviewed annually by the HR Department and Executive Leadership Team to
ensure alignment with legal requirements and business objectives.
All employees are required to acknowledge receipt and understanding of this policy within 30 days of
joining NovaTech Solutions and whenever material updates are published.

For questions regarding this policy or to report concerns, employees may contact:
hr@novatech.com
5

Title: Employee Leave Policy
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

The purpose of this Employee Leave Policy is to establish clear and consistent guidelines for leave
entitlement, leave approval, and attendance management across NovaTech Solutions. As a growing SaaS
product company with approximately 500 employees and offices in Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech
Solutions recognizes the importance of maintaining a healthy work-life balance while ensuring business
continuity and operational efficiency.
This policy defines the types of leave available to employees, eligibility requirements, approval procedures,
and employee responsibilities related to planned and unplanned absences. All employees are expected to
familiarize themselves with the provisions outlined in this document and comply with the procedures
described herein.

This policy applies to all full-time and part-time employees of NovaTech Solutions working from company
offices, remote locations, or hybrid arrangements within India.
Contractors, consultants, interns, and temporary workers may be subject to separate leave provisions as
defined in their respective agreements and are not automatically covered under this policy.

Employees are entitled to the following annual leave benefits during each calendar year (January 1 to
December 31):
Leave Type Annual Entitlement
Annual Leave (Paid Leave) 18 Days
Sick Leave 10 Days
Casual Leave 3 Days

Annual Leave is intended for vacation, personal travel, family commitments, and other planned absences.
Employees are encouraged to schedule leave in advance to minimize disruption to business operations.
1

Sick Leave may be utilized when an employee is unable to work due to illness, injury, or medical
appointments. Employees who are absent for more than three consecutive working days may be required
to provide a medical certificate upon request from HR or their Direct Manager.

Casual Leave is intended for short-term personal requirements or unforeseen circumstances that do not
qualify as Sick Leave. Casual Leave should generally be requested at least one working day in advance
whenever possible.

NovaTech Solutions allows employees to carry forward unused Annual Leave to the following calendar year,
subject to the following conditions:
• A maximum of 5 unused Annual Leave days may be carried forward.
• Carried-forward leave must be utilized by September 30 of the following year.
• Any carried-forward leave remaining after September 30 will automatically expire.
• Sick Leave and Casual Leave cannot be carried forward and do not accumulate year over year.
Employees are encouraged to utilize their leave entitlement regularly to support employee wellbeing and
productivity.

All leave requests must be submitted through the company HR portal available at:
hr-portal.novatech.com
The standard leave approval process is as follows:

section_title: 5. Employee receives confirmation through the portal and company email.
text:
For emergency absences, employees must notify their Direct Manager as soon as reasonably possible and
submit the formal leave request through the HR portal upon returning to work or when circumstances
permit.
2
NovaTech Solutions is committed to supporting employees during significant family milestones.

Eligible employees are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave in accordance with applicable laws and
company guidelines. Employees should notify their Direct Manager and the HR Manager at least eight
weeks prior to the anticipated start of maternity leave whenever possible.
Appropriate medical documentation may be required to support the leave request.

Eligible employees are entitled to 2 weeks of paid paternity leave following the birth or legal adoption of a
child.
Paternity leave should generally be taken within six months of the qualifying event and must be
coordinated with the employee's manager to ensure appropriate work coverage.

Employees are responsible for:
• Submitting leave requests accurately and in a timely manner.
• Maintaining communication with their Direct Manager regarding planned absences.
• Providing supporting documentation when requested.
• Ensuring critical work responsibilities are transitioned appropriately before extended leave periods.
• Monitoring personal leave balances through the HR portal.
Failure to follow leave procedures may result in delays in approval or classification of absences as
unauthorized.

Managers play a critical role in maintaining fair and consistent leave administration. Responsibilities
include:
• Reviewing leave requests promptly.
• Balancing employee wellbeing with business requirements.
• Ensuring equitable treatment across team members.
• Maintaining adequate staffing levels during peak business periods.
• Escalating policy-related concerns to HR when necessary.
3
Managers are expected to respond to standard leave requests within three working days whenever
possible.

The HR Department reserves the right to review leave records, request supporting documentation, and
investigate any misuse of leave benefits.
Providing false information, misrepresenting leave purposes, or repeated violations of leave procedures
may result in disciplinary action in accordance with NovaTech Solutions' Employee Code of Conduct.
NovaTech Solutions may revise this policy periodically to ensure compliance with legal requirements and
evolving business needs. Any changes will be communicated through official company channels.

For questions regarding this policy, leave balances, eligibility, or approval procedures, employees should
contact the HR Department at:
hr@novatech.com
4

Title: Employee Onboarding Guide
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

Welcome to NovaTech Solutions. We are excited to have you join our team. This Onboarding Guide has been
designed to help new employees transition smoothly into their roles and become productive members of
the organization.
As a SaaS product company with approximately 500 employees and offices in Hyderabad and Bangalore,
NovaTech Solutions believes that a structured onboarding experience is essential for employee success.
This guide outlines key activities, expectations, resources, and milestones during your first 90 days with the
company.
The onboarding process includes pre-joining preparation, orientation sessions, technology setup, team
integration, and performance milestones designed to support long-term success.

This guide applies to:
• Full-time employees
• Part-time employees
• Employees transitioning into new internal roles
• Remote and hybrid employees
• Managers responsible for onboarding new hires
Certain onboarding activities may vary depending on role, department, and location.

To ensure a smooth start, new employees should complete the following activities before their first working
day.
Employee Checklist
Task Owner Due Date
Submit signed offer letter Employee Before Joining
Complete background verification documents Employee Before Joining
1
Task Owner Due Date
Submit identification and tax documents Employee Before Joining
Upload bank account information Employee Before Joining
Complete employee information form Employee Before Joining
Review employee handbook and policies Employee Before Joining
Company Preparation Checklist
Task Owner
Create employee ID HR Team
Configure payroll records HR Team
Prepare laptop and accessories IT Team
Create email account IT Team
Provision system access IT Team
Assign onboarding buddy Hiring Manager
Schedule orientation sessions HR Team
Employees should ensure all required documentation is submitted at least three business days before their
joining date.

The first day is designed to introduce employees to NovaTech Solutions, key processes, and their team
members.
Sample Day 1 Agenda
Time Activity
9:00 AM – 9:30 AM Welcome and HR Check-In
9:30 AM – 10:30 AM Company Overview Presentation
10:30 AM – 11:00 AM Office Tour or Virtual Orientation
11:00 AM – 12:00 PM IT Setup and Account Activation
12:00 PM – 1:00 PM Team Introduction Meeting
1:00 PM – 2:00 PM Lunch with Team
2
Time Activity
2:00 PM – 3:00 PM Role Overview Discussion
3:00 PM – 4:00 PM Policy and Compliance Training
4:00 PM – 5:00 PM Goal Setting with Direct Manager
5:00 PM – 6:00 PM Q&A and Wrap-Up
Employees should arrive by 8:45 AM on their first day.

The primary objective of Week 1 is to establish foundational knowledge and familiarity with company
operations.
Week 1 Objectives

section_title: 7. Complete compliance and security training modules.
text: Employees are encouraged to actively ask questions and seek clarification whenever needed.

NovaTech Solutions provides the technology resources required for employees to perform their duties
effectively.
Initial Setup Process

3
Standard Tools
Employees may receive access to:
• Microsoft 365
• Slack
• Jira
• Confluence
• GitHub
• HR Portal
• Learning Management System (LMS)
The IT Service Desk should be contacted immediately if any system access issues occur.

Building strong relationships is an important part of the onboarding process.
New employees should meet the following individuals within their first two weeks:
Role Purpose
Direct Manager Goal setting and role expectations
HR Manager Benefits and policy guidance
Onboarding Buddy Day-to-day support
Team Members Project collaboration
Department Head Department strategy and objectives
IT Support Representative Technical assistance
Cross-Functional Stakeholders Business alignment
Managers are responsible for scheduling introductory meetings where appropriate.

All new employees are subject to a probation period of 6 months unless otherwise specified in their
employment agreement. During probation, employees are expected to:
• Demonstrate technical and professional competence. • Meet attendance and conduct standards. • Complete assigned training requirements. 4
• Contribute effectively to team objectives. • Demonstrate alignment with NovaTech Solutions values. Performance feedback will be provided regularly throughout the probation period. Probation may be confirmed, extended, or concluded based on performance and business requirements. 9. 30-Day Milestones
The first 30 days focus on learning and integration. Expected Outcomes
• Completion of onboarding activities. • Familiarity with company policies and procedures. • Understanding of role responsibilities. • Participation in team meetings. • Completion of required training programs. • Initial contributions to assigned tasks. A formal 30-day check-in meeting will be conducted with the Direct Manager. 10. 60-Day Milestones
The second month focuses on increased responsibility and independent execution. Expected Outcomes
• Ability to perform core job functions independently. • Active participation in projects. • Demonstrated understanding of business processes. • Effective collaboration with team members. • Consistent achievement of short-term goals. Managers will assess progress and identify additional development needs during the 60-day review. 11. 90-Day Milestones
By the end of the first 90 days, employees should be operating effectively within their role. Expected Outcomes
Milestone Area Expected Achievement
Productivity Consistently meeting role expectations
5
Milestone Area Expected Achievement
Technical Skills Demonstrated proficiency in required tools and processes
Team Collaboration Positive working relationships established
Performance Goals Initial objectives achieved or on track
Company Knowledge Strong understanding of products, customers, and operations
A formal 90-day review will be conducted between the employee and Direct Manager.

tive working relationships established
Performance Goals Initial objectives achieved or on track
Company Knowledge Strong understanding of products, customers, and operations
A formal 90-day review will be conducted between the employee and Direct Manager. Feedback from key stakeholders may be incorporated into the review process.

Managers play a critical role in employee onboarding and success.
Managers are expected to:
• Provide clear expectations and goals.
• Conduct weekly one-on-one meetings during the first 90 days.
• Facilitate introductions to key stakeholders.
• Monitor onboarding progress.
• Provide timely feedback and coaching.
• Support employee development and engagement.
Managers should document onboarding progress and address concerns proactively.

New employees may access support through:
• HR Department
• Direct Manager
• Onboarding Buddy
• IT Service Desk
• Learning Management System
• Employee Handbook
• Company Knowledge Base
Employees are encouraged to utilize available resources throughout the onboarding process.

This onboarding guide will be reviewed annually by the HR Department and Executive Leadership Team.
6
NovaTech Solutions may update onboarding processes, timelines, and requirements as organizational
needs evolve.
Employees will be notified of significant changes through official communication channels.

For onboarding assistance, documentation questions, system access issues, or general employment
inquiries, please contact:
hr@novatech.com
7

Title: IT Security Policy
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

The purpose of this IT Security Policy is to establish the security standards and responsibilities required to
protect the information systems, applications, networks, and data assets of NovaTech Solutions. As a SaaS
product company with approximately 500 employees operating from Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech
Solutions relies on secure technology infrastructure to serve customers, protect intellectual property, and
maintain regulatory compliance.
Cybersecurity threats continue to evolve, making it essential for all employees, contractors, and authorized
users to follow consistent security practices. This policy defines minimum security requirements for
password management, device usage, remote access, data handling, and incident reporting.
Compliance with this policy is mandatory for all personnel who access company systems, applications, or
data.

This policy applies to:
• All employees of NovaTech Solutions
• Contract workers and consultants with authorized system access
• Interns and temporary staff
• Third-party vendors accessing company resources
• All company-owned and approved personal devices used for business purposes
The policy covers:
• Laptops and desktop computers
• Mobile phones and tablets
• Cloud platforms and SaaS applications
• Internal networks and Wi-Fi systems
• Email and collaboration platforms
• Customer and company data repositories

Security is a shared responsibility across NovaTech Solutions.
1

Employees are responsible for:
• Following all security policies and procedures.
• Protecting passwords and authentication credentials.
• Reporting security incidents immediately.
• Using approved software and systems only.
• Completing mandatory annual security awareness training.

Direct Managers must ensure team compliance with security requirements and support security
investigations when necessary.

The IT Security Team is responsible for:
• Monitoring security controls.
• Managing incident response activities.
• Conducting vulnerability assessments.
• Enforcing access control standards.
• Maintaining security policies and procedures.

Strong authentication is a critical component of information security.
All users must comply with the following password standards:
Requirement Standard
Minimum Length 12 characters
Complexity Uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character
Password Reuse Last 10 passwords cannot be reused
Maximum Password Age 90 days
Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) Required for all business-critical systems
Additional requirements:
• Passwords must not contain employee names, usernames, or easily guessed information.
• Passwords must never be shared with colleagues, managers, vendors, or family members.
• Credentials must not be stored in plain text documents or unsecured notes.
2
• Approved password management tools may be used with IT Security Team approval.
Employees must immediately change passwords if compromise is suspected.

NovaTech Solutions provides company-managed devices to support business operations.
Employees using company devices must:

section_title: 5. Store company data only in approved locations.
text:
The following activities are prohibited:
• Installing unauthorized software.
• Disabling security controls.
• Jailbreaking or rooting company devices.
• Sharing devices with unauthorized individuals.
• Downloading software from unapproved sources.
Lost or stolen devices must be reported to the IT Service Desk within 1 hour of discovery.

NovaTech Solutions supports hybrid and remote work arrangements while maintaining secure access to
company resources.
Employees accessing internal systems from outside company offices must:
• Use the company-approved VPN service.
• Authenticate using Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA).
• Connect through trusted networks whenever possible.
• Ensure home Wi-Fi networks are password protected.
Remote access restrictions include:
• Public computers may not be used to access company systems.
• Customer data may not be downloaded to personal devices unless specifically authorized.
• Remote sessions may be monitored and logged for security purposes.
• VPN access may be automatically disabled after 30 consecutive days of inactivity.
The IT Security Team may revoke remote access privileges if security risks are identified.
3

To ensure consistent protection of information assets, NovaTech Solutions classifies data into three
categories.
Classification Description Examples
Information approved for external
Public Marketing materials, public website content
distribution
Information intended for
Internal Internal procedures, team documentation
employees only
Sensitive business or customer Customer records, source code, financial reports,
Confidential
information product roadmaps
Public Data
Public information may be shared externally after appropriate authorization.
Internal Data
Internal information must remain within NovaTech Solutions and should only be shared with employees or
approved business partners who require access.
Confidential Data
Confidential information requires the highest level of protection.
Employees handling confidential data must:
• Follow least-privilege access principles.
• Use encrypted storage and transmission methods.
• Avoid sharing information through personal email accounts.
• Verify recipient authorization before sharing data.
Unauthorized disclosure of confidential information may result in disciplinary action and legal
consequences.

Timely reporting is essential to minimize the impact of security incidents.
Examples of reportable incidents include:
• Suspected phishing emails
4
• Malware infections
• Lost or stolen devices
• Unauthorized access attempts
• Data leakage or accidental disclosure
• Compromised passwords
• Unusual system behavior
Employees must follow the steps below:

section_title: 6. Cooperate with investigation and remediation activities.
text:
Critical security incidents must be reported within 1 hour of discovery.
The IT Security Team will provide updates and resolution guidance throughout the incident response
process.

NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to monitor company systems, networks, email services, and business
applications to protect organizational assets and ensure policy compliance.
Employees should not expect privacy when using company-owned systems for business activities.
Security audits may be conducted quarterly, and access reviews will be performed at least twice annually.

Failure to comply with this IT Security Policy may expose NovaTech Solutions to operational, financial, legal,
and reputational risks.
Violations will be evaluated based on severity, intent, and business impact.
Potential consequences include:
Severity Level Possible Action
Minor Violation Coaching and retraining
Moderate Violation Written warning
Significant Violation Final written warning or suspension
5
Severity Level Possible Action
Serious Violation Termination of employment
Illegal Activity Referral to law enforcement authorities
Examples of serious violations include:
• Sharing credentials intentionally.
• Unauthorized disclosure of confidential customer data.
• Deliberately bypassing security controls.
• Installing malicious software.
• Unauthorized access to restricted systems.
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to pursue legal remedies where applicable.

This policy will be reviewed annually by the IT Security Team, HR Department, and Executive Leadership
Team.
Updates may be issued to address evolving threats, regulatory requirements, operational needs, or
technology changes. Employees will be notified of significant revisions and may be required to complete
refresher training within 30 days of publication.

Questions regarding this policy, security concerns, or incident reporting should be directed to:
hr@novatech.com
6

Title: NovaTech HRM Suite – Customer Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

NovaTech HRM Suite is a cloud-based Human Resource Management (HRM) platform developed by
NovaTech Solutions. The platform helps organizations manage employee records, attendance, leave
management, payroll workflows, performance reviews, onboarding, and workforce analytics through a
centralized web application.
This FAQ document addresses common customer questions related to account setup, billing, features,
troubleshooting, integrations, and account management.

What is NovaTech HRM Suite?
NovaTech HRM Suite is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform designed to simplify HR operations for small
and medium-sized businesses. The platform provides tools for employee lifecycle management, attendance
tracking, leave administration, performance management, and reporting.
Who can use the platform?
NovaTech HRM Suite is designed for:
• Human Resources teams
• Managers and Team Leads
• Payroll administrators
• Business owners
• Employees through self-service portals
Is the platform cloud-based?
Yes. NovaTech HRM Suite is fully cloud-hosted and accessible through modern web browsers without
requiring on-premises installation.
1

How do I create a new company account?
To create a company account:

Most organizations complete setup within 1–3 business days.
How many users can be added?
The number of users depends on the subscribed plan:
Plan Maximum Users
Starter 50 Users
Growth 250 Users
Business 1,000 Users
Enterprise Custom Limit
Can employee data be imported?
Yes. Administrators can upload employee records using CSV templates provided within the platform.
Supported imports include:
• Employee profiles
• Departments
• Designations
• Leave balances
• Reporting structures
Do you provide onboarding assistance?
Yes. NovaTech Solutions provides onboarding support for all paid plans. Enterprise customers receive a
dedicated Customer Success Manager during implementation.
2

What billing cycles are available?
Customers may choose:
• Monthly billing
• Annual billing
Annual subscriptions typically receive discounted pricing compared to monthly plans.
Which payment methods are supported?
Supported payment methods include:
• Credit cards
• Debit cards
• Bank transfers
• Corporate payment arrangements (Enterprise plans)
Can I upgrade my subscription?
Yes. Administrators may upgrade plans at any time through the Billing section of the Admin Console.
Plan upgrades are prorated based on the remaining subscription period.
What happens if payment fails?
If payment fails:

section_title: 4. Service suspension may occur after 30 days.
text:
Are invoices available?
Yes. Tax invoices can be downloaded from the Billing Portal for up to 24 months after issuance.

What HR functions are included?
NovaTech HRM Suite includes:
• Employee database management
3
• Leave and attendance tracking
• Employee self-service portal
• Performance management
• Document management
• Organization charts
• Workforce analytics
• Workflow automation
• Reporting dashboards
Can managers approve leave requests?
Yes. Managers can review, approve, reject, or escalate leave requests through the Manager Dashboard.
Does the system support multiple office locations?
Yes. Organizations can manage multiple offices, departments, business units, and locations within a single
account.
Is mobile access available?
Yes. Employees and managers can access the platform through iOS and Android mobile applications.
Can custom leave policies be configured?
Yes. Administrators can create customized leave policies with configurable:
• Leave types
• Accrual rules
• Carry-forward limits
• Approval workflows
• Holiday calendars

I forgot my password. What should I do?
Select the "Forgot Password" option on the login page and follow the password reset instructions sent to
your registered email address.
Why am I unable to log in?
Common causes include:
• Incorrect credentials
• Expired password
• Inactive account
• Browser cache issues
4
• Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) verification failures
If the issue persists, contact your company administrator.
The application is running slowly. How can I resolve this?
Try the following steps:

What browsers are supported?
NovaTech HRM Suite supports:
• Google Chrome (latest two versions)
• Microsoft Edge (latest two versions)
• Mozilla Firefox (latest two versions)
• Apple Safari (latest two versions)

Which integrations are supported?
NovaTech HRM Suite supports integration with:
Category Examples
Identity Providers Microsoft Entra ID, Okta
Collaboration Tools Slack, Microsoft Teams
Payroll Systems PayrollPro, PaySmart
Accounting Platforms QuickBooks, Xero
Recruitment Systems TalentFlow, RecruitMax
Is Single Sign-On (SSO) supported?
Yes. SSO is available for Business and Enterprise plans through SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols.
Are APIs available?
Yes. REST APIs are available for customers requiring custom integrations.
5
API access includes:
• Employee management
• Leave management
• Attendance records
• Reporting data
• User provisioning
Is webhook support available?
Yes. Enterprise customers can configure webhooks for real-time event notifications.

How do I add administrators?
Existing administrators can assign additional administrators through:
Settings → User Management → Roles & Permissions
Can employee permissions be customized?
Yes. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) allows organizations to configure access levels for different user
groups.
How do I deactivate an employee account?
Administrators can deactivate users from the Employee Management section. Historical records remain
available for reporting and compliance purposes.
Can company information be updated?
Yes. Authorized administrators may update:
• Company name
• Office addresses
• Business contact information
• Branding settings
• Time zone configurations
How do I cancel a subscription?
Subscription cancellations must be initiated by an account administrator through the Billing Portal.
Cancellation requests submitted at least 15 days before renewal will prevent automatic renewal of the next
billing cycle.
6

Is customer data encrypted?
Yes. NovaTech Solutions encrypts customer data both in transit and at rest using industry-standard security
controls.
Are backups performed?
Yes. Automated backups are performed daily, and backup retention is maintained for 30 days.
Does the platform support Multi-Factor Authentication?
Yes. MFA can be enabled for all users or specific user groups through the Security Settings module.
Where is customer data stored?
Customer data is hosted in secure cloud infrastructure with redundancy and disaster recovery capabilities.

How can customers contact support?
Support channels include:
Support Channel Availability
Email Support 24×7
Help Desk Portal 24×7
Live Chat Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–6:00 PM IST
Phone Support Business and Enterprise Plans
What are the support response targets?
Priority Initial Response Time
Critical Within 1 Hour
High Within 4 Hours
Medium Within 1 Business Day
Low Within 2 Business Days
7

For product support, billing assistance, technical issues, or account management inquiries, contact:
hr@novatech.com
8

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0001
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
Title: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0002
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
This Pricing & Plans Guide outlines the subscription options, billing terms, feature availability, upgrade
policies, and refund conditions for NovaTech HRM Suite, the cloud-based Human Resource Management
platform developed by NovaTech Solutions.
NovaTech HRM Suite is designed to support organizations of varying sizes, from startups and small
businesses to large enterprises. Customers can select the plan that best meets their workforce
management requirements and scale as their business grows.
All pricing listed in this document is exclusive of applicable taxes and may be updated periodically by
NovaTech Solutions with prior notice.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0003
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text: NovaTech HRM Suite offers three subscription tiers:

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0004
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text: Each tier includes increasing levels of functionality, support, automation, and customization.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0005
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
Pricing is based on the number of active users within the customer account.
Plan Monthly Billing Annual Billing (Per User/Month) Minimum Users
Starter ₹299/user/month ₹249/user/month 10 Users
Professional ₹599/user/month ₹499/user/month 25 Users
Enterprise Custom Pricing Custom Pricing 100 Users
Annual Billing Savings
Customers selecting annual billing receive approximately 17% savings compared to monthly billing rates.
1
Annual subscriptions are invoiced in advance for a 12-month term.

Custom Integrations — Limited ✓

Custom Reporting — Limited ✓

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0007
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
The Starter Plan is designed for small businesses and growing startups.
Included Features
• Employee records management
• Leave tracking
• Attendance management
2
• Employee directory
• Standard reports
• Email support
• Mobile application access
Ideal For
• Companies with 10–50 employees
• First-time HR software users
• Small businesses seeking core HR functionality
Pricing
• ₹299 per user/month (Monthly Billing)
• ₹249 per user/month (Annual Billing)

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0008
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
The Professional Plan provides advanced HR management capabilities and workflow automation tools.
Included Features
• All Starter features
• Performance management
• Goal tracking
• Automated workflows
• Advanced reporting
• API access
• SSO integration
• Enhanced permissions management
• Priority customer support
Ideal For
• Companies with 50–500 employees
• Organizations requiring automation
• Businesses with multiple departments and approval workflows
Pricing
• ₹599 per user/month (Monthly Billing)
• ₹499 per user/month (Annual Billing)

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0009
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
The Enterprise Plan is designed for large organizations requiring extensive customization, security controls,
and dedicated support.
3
Included Features
• All Professional features
• Custom integrations
• Dedicated Customer Success Manager
• Enterprise-grade security controls
• Custom implementation support
• SLA-backed support
• Sandbox environment
• Advanced compliance reporting
• Custom onboarding program
Ideal For
• Organizations with 500+ employees
• Multi-location businesses

Pricing
Enterprise pricing is customized based on:

• Integration requirements
• Support requirements
• Security and compliance needs
• Implementation scope
Customers should contact the Sales Team for a customized proposal.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0010
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
NovaTech Solutions offers flexible billing schedules.
Monthly Billing
• Invoiced monthly in advance.
• Automatically renews each month.
• User counts are reviewed monthly.
• Subscription modifications take effect in the next billing cycle.
Annual Billing
• Invoiced annually in advance.
• Provides discounted pricing.
• Locks pricing for the subscription term.
• Includes priority onboarding assistance for new customers.
4
Customers may change billing frequency at renewal periods.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0011
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
NovaTech HRM Suite offers a free trial for new customers.
Trial Details
Feature Trial Offering
Trial Duration 14 Days
User Limit Up to 25 Users
Setup Assistance Included
Credit Card Required No
Feature Access Professional Plan Features
At the end of the trial period:
• Customers may upgrade to a paid subscription.
• Trial accounts not converted within 30 days may be deleted.
• Customer data may be removed after account expiration.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0012
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
NovaTech Solutions aims to maintain transparent and fair billing practices.
Monthly Subscriptions
• Monthly subscription fees are generally non-refundable once a billing cycle has started.
• Service cancellations remain active until the end of the paid billing period.
Annual Subscriptions
• Customers may request a refund within 15 calendar days of the initial annual subscription purchase.
• Refund requests submitted after 15 days are generally not eligible for refund.
• Setup, implementation, training, and professional service fees are non-refundable.
Approved refunds will typically be processed within 15 business days.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0013
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
Customers may upgrade their subscription at any time.
5
Upgrade Process

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0014
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
section_title: 4. New features become available immediately after activation.
text:
Examples of upgrades include:
• Starter to Professional
• Professional to Enterprise
• Increasing user licenses
• Adding premium support services
No service interruption is expected during upgrades.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0015
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
Customers may downgrade plans subject to feature compatibility requirements.
Downgrade Conditions
• Downgrades become effective at the next renewal date.
• Features unavailable in the lower-tier plan will be disabled.
• Administrators are responsible for exporting data related to discontinued features before
downgrade execution.
• User counts must meet minimum plan requirements.
NovaTech Solutions may provide migration assistance where applicable.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0016
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
Customers may cancel subscriptions through the Admin Billing Portal.
Cancellation Requirements
• Monthly plans require at least 7 days' notice before renewal.
• Annual plans require at least 30 days' notice before renewal.
• Outstanding invoices must be settled before cancellation is completed.
Upon cancellation:
• Account access continues until the end of the paid subscription period.
• Customer data remains available for export for 30 days.
• Data may be permanently deleted after the retention period.
6

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0017
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to modify pricing, features, billing practices, and plan structures.
Customers will receive at least 30 days' notice before any material pricing changes affecting active
subscriptions.
Existing annual subscriptions will retain contracted pricing until the current term expires.

chunk_id: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide__0018
source_file: NovaTech HRM Suite Pricing & Plans Guide.pdf
text:
For questions regarding subscriptions, billing, pricing, upgrades, downgrades, or refunds, please contact:
hr@novatech.com
7

Title: Remote Work Policy
Version: 1.2
Effective Date: January 1, 2024
Owner: HR Department

NovaTech Solutions recognizes that flexible work arrangements can improve employee productivity, job
satisfaction, and work-life balance while supporting business objectives. As a SaaS product company with
approximately 500 employees across Hyderabad and Bangalore, NovaTech Solutions supports remote work
opportunities for eligible employees whose roles can be effectively performed outside a company office.
This Remote Work Policy establishes eligibility requirements, application procedures, performance
expectations, equipment provisions, and workplace safety standards for employees participating in remote
work arrangements.
Remote work is considered a business privilege rather than an automatic employee entitlement. Approval is
based on business needs, role suitability, performance history, and operational requirements.

This policy applies to:
• Full-time employees of NovaTech Solutions
• Eligible hybrid and fully remote workers
• Managers supervising remote employees
• Employees working from home on a temporary or permanent basis
This policy does not automatically apply to contractors, consultants, interns, or temporary workers unless
specifically approved by the HR Department and department leadership.

Not all positions are suitable for remote work. Eligibility will be determined based on job responsibilities
and business requirements.
Employees may be considered eligible if they meet the following criteria:

section_title: 2. Have achieved a performance rating of "Meets Expectations" or higher in their most recent review
text: cycle.

section_title: 5. Have a suitable home working environment with reliable internet connectivity.
6. Demonstrate the ability to work independently and maintain productivity.
text:
Roles requiring regular physical access to office facilities, specialized equipment, or frequent face-to-face
customer interaction may not qualify for full-time remote work arrangements.
Final eligibility decisions rest with the employee's Direct Manager, Department Head, and HR Department.

Employees seeking a remote work arrangement must follow the formal approval process.
Standard Approval Workflow

section_title: 5. Final approval or rejection is communicated through the HR portal.
text:
Employees must submit requests at least 10 business days before the proposed start date whenever
possible.
Approved remote work arrangements will be reviewed every 12 months to ensure continued effectiveness
and alignment with business objectives.
The company HR portal can be accessed at:
remote-work requests: hr-portal.novatech.com

Remote employees are expected to maintain the same level of professionalism, responsiveness, and
accountability as office-based employees.
Standard Working Hours
The standard work schedule at NovaTech Solutions is:
• Monday to Friday
• 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM IST
• One-hour meal break
Employees must:
• Be available during core collaboration hours from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM IST.
• Attend required meetings and team events.
2
• Maintain active status on approved communication platforms.
• Respond to internal communications within a reasonable timeframe during working hours.
Employees are expected to notify their manager when stepping away from work for extended periods
during business hours.
Repeated unavailability without approval may result in review of remote work privileges.

NovaTech Solutions provides approved equipment necessary for employees to perform their duties
effectively.
Company-Provided Equipment
Eligible remote employees may receive:
• Company-issued laptop
• External monitor (where approved)
• Keyboard and mouse
• Security software and collaboration tools
• Approved accessories required for business operations
Internet and Home Office Allowance
Eligible employees may receive:
Benefit Maximum Monthly Reimbursement
Internet Connectivity ₹2,000
Home Office Utilities ₹1,000
Reimbursements must be supported by valid invoices and submitted through the expense management
system by the 5th business day of each month.
All company-owned equipment remains the property of NovaTech Solutions and must be returned upon
termination of employment or upon request.

Employees working remotely must comply with all IT Security and Data Protection policies.
Requirements include:
• Use of company-approved VPN services.
3
• Multi-factor authentication for company systems.
• Secure storage of company devices.
• Protection of confidential information from unauthorized access.
• Immediate reporting of lost or stolen equipment.
Employees must not allow family members, friends, or other unauthorized individuals to access company
devices or information.
Failure to comply with security requirements may result in disciplinary action.

Remote employees are evaluated using the same performance standards as office-based employees.
Performance will be measured based on:
• Achievement of individual goals and objectives
• Quality of work delivered
• Productivity and output
• Customer satisfaction metrics
• Collaboration and communication effectiveness
• Adherence to deadlines and commitments
Managers are responsible for conducting regular check-ins with remote employees.
At a minimum:
• Weekly one-on-one meetings should be conducted.
• Quarterly performance reviews should include remote work effectiveness assessments.
Remote work arrangements may be modified or revoked if performance expectations are not consistently
met.

Employees are responsible for maintaining a safe and professional workspace.
Before approval, employees should verify that their workspace meets the following requirements:
Home Office Safety Checklist
• Dedicated work area with adequate lighting
• Stable desk and ergonomic chair
• Safe electrical connections and surge protection
• Reliable high-speed internet connection
• Functional smoke detector in residence
4
• Secure storage for company equipment
• Minimal workplace hazards and trip risks
• Appropriate ventilation and temperature control
• Ability to maintain confidentiality during work calls
• Emergency contact information readily available
Managers or HR representatives may request confirmation that safety requirements have been met.

Failure to comply with this policy may result in corrective action.
Examples of policy violations include:
• Misrepresentation of working hours
• Failure to maintain required availability
• Unauthorized relocation of work location
• Security policy violations
• Misuse of company equipment
• Consistent performance deficiencies
Depending on severity, disciplinary measures may include coaching, written warnings, suspension of
remote work privileges, or additional corrective action in accordance with company policies.

The HR Department and Executive Leadership Team will review this policy annually.
NovaTech Solutions reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue remote work arrangements based
on business requirements, operational changes, security concerns, or policy violations.
Employees will be notified of any material updates to this policy.

For questions regarding remote work eligibility, approvals, reimbursements, or policy interpretation, please
contact:
hr@novatech.com
5
